message:
fix(changelog): propagate JSON serialization errors instead of panicking

The robot-JSON branch of `cli::commands::changelog::execute_with_storage_ctx`
called `serde_json::to_string_pretty(&output).expect(...)` and the public
docstrings on `execute` and `execute_with_storage_ctx` carried matching
`# Panics` notes that promised "should never happen with valid data".

That guarantee is fragile — JSON serialization of `ChangelogOutput` could in
principle fail if any nested type ever returns an error from its `Serialize`
impl, and the panic would surface to the user as an unhelpful crash that
bypasses the normal error-handling path.

Replace the `expect` with `?` so the error is propagated through the
existing `Result` return type and surfaces as a normal CLI error. Update
the docstrings on both `execute` and `execute_with_storage_ctx` to drop
the `# Panics` section and add "or output serialization fails" to the
`# Errors` section, keeping documentation honest about the new behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
